tracks.label: Classification Lab
tracks.description: Compare classification methods across evaluation, interpretability, and data requirements

methods.name: Logistic Regression
methods.what_it_does: Models probability of a binary outcome using a linear combination of features passed through a sigmoid function.
methods.when_to_use: When you need interpretable coefficients, have linearly separable classes, or need probability outputs.
methods.interpretability: High — coefficients are directly interpretable as log-odds
methods.data_requirements: Works well on small to medium datasets; sensitive to multicollinearity
methods.common_confusion: Treating logistic regression as a classification algorithm that outputs a hard class, not a probability

methods.name: Decision Tree
methods.what_it_does: Partitions feature space into rectangular regions using a series of binary splits on feature thresholds.
methods.when_to_use: When you need explainability, have categorical features, or want a model that doesn't require feature scaling.
methods.interpretability: Very high — tree diagram is directly readable by stakeholders
methods.data_requirements: Handles mixed types well; prone to overfitting without pruning
methods.common_confusion: Confusing a single decision tree with ensemble tree methods like Random Forest

methods.name: Random Forest
methods.what_it_does: Aggregates predictions from many decorrelated decision trees trained on bootstrapped subsets of data and features.
methods.when_to_use: When prediction accuracy matters more than interpretability; robust to outliers and noisy features.
methods.interpretability: Low to medium — feature importances exist but individual decisions are opaque
methods.data_requirements: Works well on medium to large datasets; robust to missing values
methods.common_confusion: Assuming bagging alone explains Random Forest — feature subsampling at each split is what creates decorrelation

methods.name: Gradient Boosting
methods.what_it_does: Builds an additive model by sequentially fitting shallow trees to the residuals (gradients) of previous trees.
methods.when_to_use: When you need top predictive performance on tabular data and have sufficient data to avoid overfitting.
methods.evaluation_metric: AUC-ROC, log-loss; early stopping monitors validation loss
methods.interpretability: Low — SHAP values can explain predictions post-hoc
methods.data_requirements: Sensitive to hyperparameters; requires tuning of learning rate, depth, n_estimators
methods.common_confusion: Conflating boosting (sequential, residual correction) with bagging (parallel, variance reduction)

tracks.label: Optimization Lab
tracks.description: Compare optimization formulations and solution methods for business decisions

methods.name: Linear Programming
methods.what_it_does: Optimizes a linear objective function subject to linear equality and inequality constraints.
methods.when_to_use: When decision variables are continuous and both the objective and constraints are linear.
methods.evaluation_metric: Optimal objective value, shadow prices, sensitivity ranges
methods.interpretability: High — dual variables (shadow prices) directly quantify constraint value
methods.data_requirements: Requires explicit formulation of objective and constraints; scales well with simplex method
methods.common_confusion: Applying LP when variables must be integer — LP relaxation may give fractional (infeasible) solutions

methods.name: Integer Programming
methods.what_it_does: Extends LP by requiring some or all decision variables to take integer values, enabling binary yes/no decisions.
methods.when_to_use: When decisions are discrete: assign/not assign, open/close facility, select/reject project.
methods.evaluation_metric: Optimal integer objective, optimality gap, branch-and-bound nodes explored
methods.interpretability: High for formulation; computation is NP-hard in the worst case
methods.data_requirements: Same as LP but computationally much harder; heuristics or solvers needed for large instances
methods.common_confusion: Assuming you can round an LP relaxation solution to get a feasible integer solution — rounding can violate constraints

methods.name: Dynamic Programming
methods.what_it_does: Solves multi-stage decision problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems and storing solutions.
methods.when_to_use: When a problem has optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems — sequential or staged decisions.
methods.interpretability: Medium — the recursion logic is clear but can be hard to communicate to non-technical audiences
methods.data_requirements: State space must be enumerable; curse of dimensionality limits scalability
methods.common_confusion: Confusing DP with greedy algorithms — DP considers all possibilities; greedy commits to locally optimal choices

tracks.label: Database Design Lab
tracks.description: Compare data modeling approaches, normal forms, and schema design patterns

methods.name: Conceptual Modeling (ERD)
methods.what_it_does: Captures entities, their attributes, and relationships at a business level — independent of implementation.
methods.when_to_use: At the start of database design to align with stakeholders before committing to a physical schema.
methods.evaluation_metric: Completeness of entity coverage, cardinality correctness, absence of redundancy
methods.interpretability: Very high — entity-relationship diagrams communicate directly with business users
methods.data_requirements: Requires domain knowledge; no data needed — it models business rules
methods.common_confusion: Confusing conceptual ERD (business-level) with logical or physical schema (implementation-level)

methods.name: Normalization (1NF–3NF)
methods.what_it_does: Organizes relational tables to eliminate redundancy and update anomalies through normal form decomposition.
methods.when_to_use: During logical database design to ensure data integrity before physical implementation.
methods.evaluation_metric: Elimination of transitive and partial dependencies; update/insert/delete anomaly freedom
methods.interpretability: High — rules are systematic and auditable
methods.data_requirements: Requires a candidate key and functional dependency analysis of the data domain
methods.common_confusion: Over-normalizing to BCNF/4NF when 3NF is sufficient for most OLTP applications
